prose.rule: DELIVERY is the ONE moment an agent crosses from inside the digital world to the outside real world. It is literalized as the Chief of Agents peeking out of a MacBook: MOSTLY INSIDE the screen, with only its face and hands (holding the finished, sealed work) reaching out into the real room to hand it to the human. Legs and most of the body stay inside the laptop. The crossing happens exactly ONCE per delivery; nowhere else does a digital being enter the real world.
prose.source: Locked with Gary 2026-07-16 (chief-delivering-v4, then the isolated delivering.png).
